sources.data: A goblin is a diminutive, grotesque, and often malevolent humanoid creature prominent in European folklore, characterized by mischievous or demonic nature, small stature, and ability to shapeshift. Etymology derives from Old French gobelin (12th century), possibly linked to Medieval Latin gobelinus or Greek kobalos (rogue or sprite). Similar creatures include brownies, dwarves, duendes, gnomes, imps, leprechauns, and kobolds. In English/Scottish folklore, goblins appear as brownies - benevolent household spirits that perform domestic chores. Goblins are believed to dwell in subterranean areas or households, engaging in acts ranging from pranks to murder.

'Goblin mode' is a neologism for the rejection of societal expectations in a hedonistic manner without concern for one's self-image. The term went viral in early 2022 after a tweet featuring a doctored Fox News headline. In December 2022, it was Oxford Languages' Word of the Year. The popularity may be linked to rejection of curated lifestyles on social media and coping with COVID-19 pandemic effects.
